name: El Grado and Hoz de Barbastro - circular route
name_original: El Grado y Hoz de Barbastro – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around El Grado offers a diverse landscape characterized by riverine environments, varied topography, and significant elevation changes. The region features the Cubia and Nalón rivers, providing accessible routes along their banks. Cyclists encounter a mix of ancient pathways and modern roads, with options ranging from mostly flat sections to routes with considerable ascents. This area provides a range of terrain suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around El Grado The most popular road cycling route is El Grado and Hoz de Barbastro - circular route, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete, featuring a mix of open roads and scenic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puente de Abajo – Capella Romanesque Bridge loop from Estada, a difficult 55.3 miles (89.0 km) path. This route includes substantial elevation gains and passes through varied rural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the El Grado Dam – Ubiergo Village loop from El Grado - Lo Grau, a 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and past the El Grado Dam, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Road cycling around El Grado is defined by river valleys, ancient pathways, and diverse topography with notable elevation gains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in El Grado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Grado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around El Grado?
A: El Grado offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 75 different tours to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in El Grado?
A: The terrain around El Grado is quite diverse, characterized by riverine environments, ancient pathways, and varied topography. You'll find routes along the Cubia and Nalón rivers, as well as sections with significant elevation changes, offering a mix of flat stretches and considerable climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: Yes, El Grado has options for all skill levels. There are 9 easy road cycling routes available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These often follow river valleys, providing a gentler experience.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in El Grado?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, El Grado offers 33 difficult routes with substantial elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40317406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo – Capella Romanesque Bridge loop from Estada&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 55.3 miles (89.0 km) and includes significant ascents through varied rural landscapes.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in El Grado?
A: The months of July and August are particularly popular for road cycling in El Grado, offering generally favorable weather conditions for exploring the region's diverse routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in El Grado?
A: Yes, many routes in El Grado are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924193454/el-grado-and-hoz-de-barbastro-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Grado and Hoz de Barbastro - circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.5-mile (42.6 km) trail with scenic views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in El Grado?
A: Road cycling routes in El Grado often pass by stunning natural features. You can encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Torreciudad Sanctuary and the Grado I reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4990014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barasona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes may also lead you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozán de Vero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or unique geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portal de Cunarga: Stone Arch&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical routes or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike in El Grado?
A: El Grado is rich in history, offering routes that trace ancient paths. You can explore sections of the **Camín Real de la Mesa**, an old Roman road, or the **Ruta de la Escanda (Spelt Route)**, which highlights the region's agricultural heritage. The historic center of Grado itself, with its 18th-century architecture and local marketplace, is also worth exploring.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in El Grado?
A: While many routes involve elevation, there are easier options suitable for families, especially those following the river valleys. These routes tend to be less strenuous and offer a pleasant way to experience the natural beauty of the area together.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in El Grado?
A: The road cycling routes in El Grado are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, scenic views, and the variety of challenges available.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the road cycling routes in El Grado?
A: El Grado, as a river town with a historic center, offers various amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town and surrounding villages, providing convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays during your cycling adventures.

Q: Are there routes that offer significant elevation gains for training?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in El Grado feature substantial elevation gains, making them ideal for training or challenging rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo – Capella Romanesque Bridge loop from Barasona&lt;/a&gt; covers over 100 km with more than 1500 meters of ascent.
tips: Naval (Nabal in Aragonese) has a population of 270 inhabitants (INE 2017); Due to its geographical location at the foot of the Sierra de Arbe, it was until 1989, the year of the construction of a new route on the A-138 road that avoided passing through the Alto del Pino pass and therefore through the Villa de Naval, the natural way of communication of the Somontano with the Sobrarbe and with France.

Hoz de Barbastro (in Aragonese Oz de Balbastro) is a town belonging to the municipality of Hoz y Costean. Its name comes from the vulgar Latin fox, focis (in classical Latin faux, faucis), which means throat, gorge. At 704 m above sea level. Located near the Barranco de la Salina on a small mound.

Locality of El Grado that in 2018 had 27 inhabitants. Leaving El Grado, it runs parallel to the Cinca canal along a very rough road, almost more for gravel than for road. Then, on the way to Hoz de Barbastro, the road improves a bit.
